page: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng testing, while multi-core CPU performance falls in the middle 50%. The memory subsystem is a key highlight, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10%, although uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier at 59.63 GB/sec and 34.57 GB/sec respectively. In language model inference, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 300.3 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, but drops to a poor 4.99 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

Given its massive memory allocation relative to compute cores, this server offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without requiring the payment overhead of additional, unneeded vCPUs. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled and depend on attached network volumes. This profile is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications with massive memory footprints, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz. It is configured with an exceptionally high memory capacity of 672.0 GB, providing 28.0 GB of RAM per core, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. This memory-optimized profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring vast memory footprints without proportional compute scaling. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring a 24-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 672.0 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 24 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, designed for large in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC processor and an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores
672.0 GB of RAM providing a high 28.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier memory performance ranking in the top 10% of PassMark benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and caching layers
